title: Identify Main Components At The Operator Station
summary: Use the operator station overview, Figure 3-1, and Table 3-1 from the manual to locate and identify the documented operator station components in the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when a user needs to visually identify the documented operator station components and compare the physical station to the manual's listed features.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to operate the HMI.
Do not use this procedure to operate the push-buttons.
Do not infer component names or locations beyond what is stated in the section text, Figure 3-1 metadata, and Table 3-1.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Visually locate and identify the documented operator station components using the manual text, Figure 3-1, and Table 3-1.

safety_notes: This runbook is a visual identification reference only.
Do not infer component names or locations beyond what is stated in the source.
Escalate if the physical station does not match the documented feature list or figure.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access or visual access to the operator station
Figure 3-1 Operator Station (Panels Removed for Clarity)

access_required: Physical access or visual access to the operator station
system_preconditions: The user can view the operator station area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers.
external_references: Figure 3-1 Operator Station (Panels Removed for Clarity)

visual_references.description: Primary reference photo of the operator station with panels removed, showing the main operator-facing components.
Supplemental photo of the rotary station referenced as an operator station component.
Supplemental diagram for identifying tipper assemblies.
Supplemental artifact showing an operator station HMI screen layout.
Supplemental artifact showing an operator station HMI screen layout.

steps.title: Go to the operator station area
steps.purpose: Establish the correct physical area for component identification.
steps.instruction: Go to the operator station, defined by the manual as the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers.
steps.expected_result: You are positioned at the documented operator station area.
steps.healthy_condition: The area matches the operator station description in the manual.
steps.failure_condition: The area cannot be confirmed as the operator station described in the manual.

actions.label: Locate operator station area
actions.exact_action: Go to the operator station, the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall operator station layout and the area where the main components are shown together.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical area does not match the documented operator station description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The operator station is the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers.
Figure 3-1, Operator Station (Panels Removed for Clarity), is a reference view of the operator station.

steps.title: Identify the two tippers
steps.purpose: Confirm the presence of the two tippers listed as part of the operator station.
steps.instruction: Identify the two tippers at the operator station using the documented feature list and Figure 3-1.
steps.expected_result: Two tippers are identified at the operator station.
steps.healthy_condition: Two tippers are visible and consistent with the manual description.
steps.failure_condition: Two tippers cannot be identified or the station does not match the documented layout.

actions.label: Identify tippers
actions.exact_action: Identify the two tippers at the operator station using the documented feature list.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tipper locations in the overall operator station view.
Tipper assembly appearance and labeled tipper components for supplemental identification.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical station does not match the documented feature list or figure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In addition to containing two tippers, the operator station includes a chute, plastic curtains, rotary station, HMI screen, and four push-buttons.
Table 3-1: Operator Station Features lists Tipper, Chute, Stacklight, Plastic curtain, Operator station HMI, Push-buttons, and Rotary station.
Use this artifact to retrieve component names, physical layout, and operator-station hardware references.
This maintenance diagram supports identification of left tipper assembly parts.

steps.expected_result: The chute is identified at the operator station.
steps.healthy_condition: The chute is visible and consistent with the documented operator station layout.
steps.failure_condition: The chute cannot be identified or does not match the documented layout.

steps.title: Locate the two stacklights
steps.purpose: Identify the stacklights using the source's stated mounting location.
steps.instruction: Locate the two stacklights mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
steps.expected_result: Two stacklights are identified in the documented mounting positions.
steps.healthy_condition: Two stacklights are visible on the top of the frame, one on each side of the chute.
steps.failure_condition: The stacklights cannot be identified in the documented positions or the station layout does not match the source.

actions.label: Locate stacklights
actions.exact_action: Locate the two stacklights mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The top of the frame on either side of the chute where the two stacklights are documented.

stop_or_escalate_if: Two stacklights are not visible in the documented positions.
The physical station does not match the documented feature list or figure.

steps.title: Locate the push-buttons
steps.purpose: Identify the push-button area and confirm the documented quantity.
steps.instruction: Locate the push-buttons at the operator station and verify that the section states there are four push-buttons.
steps.expected_result: The push-buttons are identified and the documented quantity is recognized as four.
steps.healthy_condition: The push-button area is identified and aligns with the manual's statement that there are four push-buttons.
steps.failure_condition: The push-buttons cannot be identified or the observed station does not match the documented quantity and layout.

steps.title: Compare observed components to Table 3-1
steps.purpose: Verify that the observed station features match the documented operator station feature list.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed components to Table 3-1 to verify that the station features match the documented list: tipper, chute, stacklight, plastic curtain, operator station HMI, push-buttons, and rotary station.
steps.expected_result: The observed operator station components match the documented feature list.
steps.healthy_condition: All observed components align with Table 3-1 and Figure 3-1.
steps.failure_condition: One or more observed components do not match the documented feature list or figure.

actions.label: Verify documented feature list
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed components to Table 3-1 to verify that the station features match the documented list.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall station layout for comparison against the documented feature list.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical station does not match the documented feature list or figure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-1: Operator Station Features lists Tipper, Chute, Stacklight, Plastic curtain, Operator station HMI, Push-buttons, and Rotary station.
Figure 3-1 shows the operator station with panels removed for clarity.

success_criteria: The user can identify the documented operator station components: tipper, chute, stacklight, plastic curtain, operator station HMI, push-buttons, and rotary station.
The observed station features match the documented list in Table 3-1 and the layout shown in Figure 3-1.
healthy_conditions: The operator station is identifiable as the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers.
Two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
The operator station HMI is identifiable.
Four push-buttons are documented and the push-button area is identifiable.
Observed components match Table 3-1 and Figure 3-1.
failure_conditions: The physical station does not match the documented feature list or figure.
A listed component cannot be identified from the physical station and supplied source evidence.
The user must infer component names or locations beyond what is stated in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the physical station does not match the documented feature list or figure.
Escalate if any listed component cannot be confidently identified using the supplied source evidence.
Do not infer undocumented component names, locations, or functions.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 3-1 shows the operator station with panels removed for clarity.

The operator station is the area where an operator receives parcels from the tippers. In addition to containing two tippers, the operator station includes a chute, plastic curtains, rotary station, HMI screen, and four push-buttons. Two stacklights are mounted on the top of the frame on either side of the chute.
Table 3-1: Operator Station Features lists Tipper, Chute, Stacklight, Plastic curtain, Operator station HMI, Push-buttons, and Rotary station.
